billywoods: generally: the only way for me to win a high-handicap game is to play moves that are a little too greedy to work, in the hope that you won't be able to refute them (e.g. a proper refutation would drag us into complicated fights where i have the advantage)
billywoods: as such, the way for you to win is to not allow me to get too greedy with my moves and to be prepared to fight me

billywoods: (or, of course, if you think a fight is too complicated, then you can back out of it and let me take a bit more than i deserve, but where possible do so while not losing too much and while retaining sente and so on)
billywoods: locally, at least, as you can probably tell, my aim is to connect my little L-shaped group to the outside, either the centre or that stone on g16

billywoods: so instead i played that hane that allowed you to cut, knowing that - if you did - a fight would probably break out. now my L-shaped group is weak and detached, but also your groups nearby have clumsy shape and cutting points and so on, so maybe i can get something out of that. who knows.

billywoods: here are my thoughts, in case you find them helpful:

billywoods: this looks to me to be easily the 'biggest' thing on the board in a sense. if A is dead, then you have 7 captured stones and about 11 points of territory there, whereas if B and C die, i have 15 captured stones and 20 points of territory
billywoods: that's a swing of ~55 points, so it's pretty huge :)

billywoods: of course, if you think those groups are already decided (in either direction) and you don't need to spend a move there, that's fine too. i haven't played there because i think they're decided
billywoods: your x group is surrounded by my circle group (which, note, can't be disconnected), and the circle group is big, has lots of space for two eyes, and has lots of liberties
billywoods: (also, if your clump in the middle is dead, then circle will end upconnected to triangle)
billywoods: which means that, if you want x to live, you have to make it live somewhere in the space it has
billywoods: but the picture is misleading. how valuable it is to play in each of these areas doesn't actually depend on their relative sizes, but rather on how sente / gote they are
